2606.14209.title: Detecting undisclosed LLM-generated content in parliamentary texts
2606.14209.paper_summary: In this paper, we evaluate the extent of undisclosed LLM-generated content in texts from the parliaments of the United Kingdom and Sweden. In many areas, such as in journalism or in academic writing, there are often requirements to clearly disclose whether AI tools, such as LLMs, have been used. In the case of parliamentary texts, the guidelines on disclosure of AI use are more vague. However, in order to maintain transparency and retain public trust, it is generally recommended that parliamentarians should state whether or not they have used AI when writing texts, such as parliamentary motions. Here, we train an interpretable (glass-box) text classifier using pre-LLM parliamentary texts and LLM-generated versions of such texts. We then apply the classifier to a test set containing recent parliamentary texts, finding a steady increase in undisclosed LLM use, in both parliaments, from 2022 onwards.
2606.14209.paper_summary_zh: 在本文中，我們評估來自英國和瑞典國會的文本中未披露的LLM生成內容的程度。<lb/>在許多領域，例如新聞業或學術寫作，通常有要求明確披露是否使用了AI工具，如LLM。<lb/>在國會文本的情況下，對於AI使用的披露指導方針則較為模糊。<lb/>然而，為了維持透明度並保留公眾信任，通常建議國會議員在撰寫文本（如國會動議）時應聲明是否使用了AI。<lb/>在這裡，我們使用前LLM國會文本和這些文本的LLM生成版本訓練了一個可解釋的（玻璃盒）文本分類器。<lb/>然後，我們將該分類器應用於包含近期國會文本的測試集，發現自2022年起，兩個國會中未披露的LLM使用情況穩步增加。
2606.14209.author: Minerva Suvanto et.al.
2606.14209.authors: Minerva Suvanto, Andrea McGlinchey, Peter J. Barclay, Mattias Wahde
